answer:
\boxed{C}

On the cello and double bass, the **pinky finger** (ring finger) typically rests on top of the bow to provide stability and control, especially for heavier strokes. In contrast, violin and viola players usually place their **forefinger** (index) on top of the bow, leaving the pinky free for other functions. This difference reflects the larger bow size and greater weight involved in cello/bass playing.
